mechanism: The ~200bp asymmetry in the Fed's reaction function (easing at ~100bp above target, tightening at ~300bp) produces a de facto 2.3-2.8% long-run average that both reflects and reinforces the structural upward shift in inflation. The reaction function and the structural shift are mutually sustaining.
regime_dependence: Holds under current FOMC composition and political economy; could change with framework review, personnel changes, or a severe inflation scare
